JS Core (72) - ES6 Tagged Template Literals: The Backtick Trick You've Never Used
ES6 Template Literals

Template literals are the backtick strings you use every day. But there's a more advanced form hiding in there — tagged template literals. This post walks through how they work, why the output looks so weird at first, and two practical uses: bulk-wrapping values in HTML and preventing XSS attacks.
